title: Broad SMA Cross Reentry
hypothesis: An absolute-trend SMA-cross family can test a materially different broad-universe thesis after both momentum and mean-reversion branches retired without clearing promotion gates.
why_different_from_retired: The retired broad branches relied on cross-sectional ranking of recent winners or losers, while this family uses per-instrument absolute trend confirmation from a short-vs-long moving-average crossover.
This branch changes both the signal family and the evidence shape: it only admits names already in confirmed upward trends instead of forcing relative selection from a weak cross-sectional field.
success_criteria: The SMA-cross seed must pass the existing validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion policy without exceptions before it can move toward paper-trade preparation.
If it fails, it must still produce enough evidence to retire absolute-trend reentry as a family instead of leaving the governed queue in a stale state.

stop_conditions.when: The SMA-cross seed passes validation, holdout, and walk-forward promotion rules under the current policy.
The seed records a terminal promotion decision and still fails the policy, especially on benchmark-relative split or walk-forward evidence.

bootstrap.objective: Run one bounded absolute-trend family on the broad universe and decide whether SMA-cross trend confirmation deserves promotion-path follow-up or retirement.
bootstrap.branch_rationale: The governed queue is blocked because both the broad momentum and broad mean-reversion branches retired. The repo already supports SMA-cross scoring, so this is the cleanest materially different successor family that can run without new model work.
positive_hypotheses: Absolute-trend confirmation can avoid the benchmark-relative weakness that persisted when the system was forced to rank relative winners or losers across the broad universe.
A slower 20/100 trend filter with a modest confirmation threshold may produce a smaller but more durable candidate set than the exhausted short-horizon mean-reversion branch.

seed_stack.label: Retired broad momentum control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference control for the exhausted broad-momentum family.

seed_stack.label: Retired broad mean-reversion control
seed_stack.rationale: Reference control for the retired first non-momentum branch.

seed_stack.label: SMA-cross seed
seed_stack.rationale: Repo-backed absolute-trend family using the existing SMA-cross strategy with a broad-universe profile identity.

campaign_path.label: SMA-Cross Seed
campaign_path.purpose: Execute the first governed absolute-trend family and decide whether it should be promoted or retired.
